en.extract:
The citron-crested cockatoo (Cacatua citrinocristata) is a medium-sized cockatoo with an orange crest, dark grey beak, pale orange ear patches, and strong feet and claws. The underside of the larger wing and tail feathers has a pale yellow color. The eyelid color is a very light blue. Both sexes are similar. Females have a copper colored eye where as the male has a very dark black eye.
They are endemic to Sumba in the Lesser Sunda Islands in Indonesia. The diet consists mainly of seeds, buds, fruits, nuts and herbaceous plants.
In 2022, Birdlife International recognized the citron-crested cockatoo as a separate species, Cacatua citrinocristata, assessed as Critically Endangered, while the International Ornithological Congress designated it to be a species in 2023.


Conservation status

The citron-crested cockatoo is a critically endangered bird whose population has declined due to habitat loss and illegal trapping for the cage-bird trade. A 1993 survey of Sumba estimated the species' numbers at less than 2,000 individuals. By 2012, the estimate had dropped to 563, and as of 2019 the population numbers are estimated to be 1,200-2,000 individuals with 800-1,320 mature individuals.  It is listed in appendix I of the CITES list. Consequently, international trade is strongly regulated and trade in wild caught citron-crested cockatoos is illegal.


References


External links

The '''citron-crested cockatoo''' ('''''Cacatua citrinocristata''''') is a medium-sized [[cockatoo]] with an orange [[Crest (feathers)|crest]], dark grey [[beak]], pale orange ear patches, and strong feet and claws. The underside of the larger wing and tail [[feather]]s has a pale yellow color. The eyelid color is a very light blue. Both sexes are similar. Females have a copper colored eye where as the male has a very dark black eye.

They are [[Endemism in birds|endemic]] to [[Sumba]] in the [[Lesser Sunda Islands]] in [[Indonesia]]. The diet consists mainly of seeds, buds, fruits, nuts and herbaceous plants.

In 2022, [[Birdlife International]] recognized the citron-crested cockatoo as a separate species, ''Cacatua citrinocristata'', assessed as Critically Endangered,<ref name="birdlife">{{cite web |title=BirdLife International (2022) Species factsheet: Cacatua citrinocristata |url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/citron-crested-cockatoo-cacatua-citrinocristata |publisher=Birdlife International |access-date=5 March 2022}}</ref> while the [[International Ornithological Congress]] designated it to be a species in 2023.<ref name="ioc">{{cite web |title=Parrots, cockatoos |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/parrots/ |website=IOC World Bird List |access-date=18 July 2023}}</ref>

==Conservation status==
[[File:Cacatua sulphurea citrinocristata kakadu žlutolící ZOO Děčín.jpg|upright|left|thumb|Chick at [[Děčín]] Zoo, Czech Republic]]
The citron-crested cockatoo is a critically endangered bird whose population has declined due to [[habitat loss]] and illegal trapping for the cage-bird trade. A 1993 survey of Sumba estimated the species' numbers at less than 2,000 individuals.<ref name=r1/> By 2012, the estimate had dropped to 563, and as of 2019 the population numbers are estimated to be 1,200-2,000 individuals with 800-1,320 mature individuals.<ref name=IUCN>{{Cite iucn|title=Citron-crested Cockatoo|author=[[BirdLife International]]|year=2021|article-number=e.T200296433A201545199|doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2021-3.RLTS.T200296433A201545199.en|access-date=15 December 2023}}</ref>  It is listed in appendix I of the [[CITES]] list.<ref name=r2/> Consequently, international trade is strongly regulated and trade in wild caught citron-crested cockatoos is illegal.

zh.extract:
橘冠凤头鹦鹉（學名：Cacatua citrinocristata，英語：Citron-crested cockatoo），又称浅黄冠凤头鹦鹉，是鸚形目鳳頭鸚鵡科鳳頭鸚鵡屬的一種鳥類。該物種僅分布于印度尼西亚的松巴島內。因遭受了劇烈的族群下降，在《國際自然保護聯盟瀕危物種紅色名錄》中被列為極危物種。
這種鳥類由英國動物學家路易斯·弗雷澤首次以「Plyctolophus citrino-cristatus（原文如此）」的名字命名，並只用了簡短的描述寫明該物種是在倫敦動物學會的籠舍中發現的。
在過去，該物種一直被視為是小葵花鳳頭鸚鵡的亞種。在2004年的測量研究中，其頭冠的顏色、尾長比例、性情均與其他亞種有所不同，因此被認為擁有「相當接近物種的地位」。2021年時，國際鳥盟將其獨立為一物種，而世界鳥類學家聯合會也在2023年跟隨其作法。
其屬名Cacatua來自馬來語Kakatiid或Kakak-tud，這些詞即指這些鳳頭鸚鵡。種小名citrinocristata源自拉丁語的citrinus（意為「檸檬黃」）及cristatus（意為「帶有頭飾的」）。
這種鳳頭鸚鵡体长33厘米，除了擁有橘黃色（或橙杏色）的頭冠之外，翅膀及尾巴內側也有一些淡黃色羽毛。牠們主要以水果、浆果、花朵、坚果种子为食，饲养環境下也能食用蔬菜等綠色食物。喙强壮而有力，因此饲养需要关在金属笼子内，同时主要经常陪伴，非则无聊过度会啄伤自己生气时会竖起羽冠。
其繁殖季在5—9月之間。二到四岁后达到性成熟，每次产卵2—3颗，孵化期3—4周，羽化期8—10周，每年可产卵两次。其繁殖成功率可能相當受到雨量的影響，並且非常偏好相當高的大型落葉樹（平均高度約22公尺）。
松巴島現在正一直不斷遭受到樹林砍伐的破壞，其森林大多被碎片化，並影響了橘冠鳳頭鸚鵡的數量。同時這個物種在歷史上也一直被視為農業上的有害生物，加上現在大量被寵物貿易及用以食用，在過去約50年中可能減少了80%的數量，並在2021年時推估餘下不到2000隻。《瀕危野生動植物種國際貿易公約》也將其列為附錄一的名錄中，印尼政府也已立法將其列入完全的保護中。
